nodes.name: World Leaders
nodes.description: Heads of state and government worldwide (as of 2025)

items.prompt: Who is the current President of the United States?
items.explanation:
47th President (2025–present), Republican
Previously served as the 45th President (2017–2021), making him the second president to serve non-consecutive terms (after Grover Cleveland)
Defeated Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election
First president to be convicted of felony crimes (2024 New York hush-money trial) before winning reelection
Impeached twice during his first term (2019, 2021)—acquitted by the Senate both times
Former real estate developer and host of The Apprentice
At 78 years old at inauguration, the oldest person ever inaugurated as president

items.prompt: Who is the current President of France?
items.explanation:
President of France (2017–present)
Youngest president in French history, elected at age 39
Founded the centrist party En Marche! (now Renaissance) in 2016
Married to Brigitte Macron, who was his drama teacher—24 years his senior
Reelected in 2022, defeating Marine Le Pen for the second time
Former investment banker at Rothschild & Co and Minister of the Economy under Hollande
Faced prolonged Yellow Vest protests (2018–2019) and pension reform strikes (2023)

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom?
items.explanation:
Prime Minister (2024–present), Labour Party
Took office July 5, 2024, after Labour won a landslide general election, ending 14 years of Conservative rule
Former Director of Public Prosecutions (2008–2013), head of the Crown Prosecution Service
Knighted in 2014 for services to law and criminal justice (hence "Sir Keir")
Leader of the Labour Party since 2020, succeeding Jeremy Corbyn
First Labour PM since Gordon Brown (2007–2010)
Named after Keir Hardie, the first leader of the Labour Party

items.prompt: Who is the current Chancellor of Germany?
items.explanation:
Chancellor of Germany (May 2025–present), CDU/CSU
Elected chancellor by the Bundestag on May 6, 2025
Leader of the Christian Democratic Union (CDU) since 2022
Won the snap federal election on February 23, 2025, after Olaf Scholz's coalition collapsed
Former corporate lawyer and BlackRock Germany board member
Lost the CDU leadership to Angela Merkel in 2002 and spent years in the political wilderness before returning
Forms a coalition government; succeeded Olaf Scholz (SPD) as chancellor

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of Canada?
items.explanation:
24th Prime Minister (March 2025–present), Liberal Party
Sworn in March 14, 2025, five days after winning the Liberal leadership with 85.9% of the vote
Succeeded Justin Trudeau, who resigned as Liberal leader in January 2025
Former Governor of the Bank of Canada (2008–2013) and Governor of the Bank of England (2013–2020)
First Canadian to lead a central bank of another G7 country
Had never held elected office before becoming PM—won the April 2025 federal election
Campaigned largely on confronting U.S. tariffs under the Trump administration

items.prompt: Who is the current President of Brazil?
items.explanation:
39th President of Brazil (2023–present), Workers' Party (PT)
Previously served two terms (2003–2010); returned to office 12 years later
Was a metalworker and union leader with no university degree—rose to lead Latin America's largest country
Imprisoned for corruption in 2018; convictions annulled in 2021 on jurisdictional grounds
Defeated incumbent Jair Bolsonaro in the 2022 runoff by a narrow margin
Commonly known simply as "Lula," which was legally added to his name
Held the BRICS presidency in 2025

items.prompt: Who is the current President of Mexico?
items.explanation:
66th President of Mexico (October 2024–present), Morena party
First woman and first Jewish person to serve as President of Mexico
Won the 2024 election with nearly 60% of the vote—the highest percentage since free elections began in Mexico
Holds a PhD in energy engineering from UNAM; was a contributing author on IPCC climate reports
Former Mayor of Mexico City (2018–2023)
Protege of predecessor Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador (AMLO); continues his "Fourth Transformation" agenda
Carried 31 of 32 states in her election victory

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
items.explanation:
Prime Minister of India (2014–present), Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)
Won three consecutive general elections (2014, 2019, 2024)
Former Chief Minister of Gujarat (2001–2014), India's longest-serving CM of that state
Promoted "Make in India" industrialization and the Digital India initiative
Once denied a U.S. visa over the 2002 Gujarat riots; now one of the world's most-traveled leaders
Oversaw India's G20 presidency in 2023 and the Chandrayaan-3 moon landing
Highest domestic approval rating among world leaders, consistently near 70%

items.prompt: Who is the current President of China?
items.explanation:
President of the People's Republic of China (2013–present), General Secretary of the CPC (2012–present)
Most powerful Chinese leader since Mao Zedong
Removed presidential term limits in 2018, allowing indefinite rule
Secured an unprecedented third term as General Secretary in 2022
Architect of the Belt and Road Initiative, China's global infrastructure program
Father Xi Zhongxun was a revolutionary leader; Xi was sent to rural labor during the Cultural Revolution
Centralized power more than any Chinese leader in decades, including a sweeping anti-corruption campaign

items.prompt: Who is the current President of Russia?
items.explanation:
President of Russia (2000–2008, 2012–present)
Served as Prime Minister (2008–2012) between presidential terms to comply with consecutive-term limits
Former KGB intelligence officer stationed in Dresden, East Germany
Ordered the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, triggering the largest European land war since WWII
Won the 2024 presidential election with 87% of the vote in a contest widely regarded as neither free nor fair
Overseen constitutional changes (2020) potentially allowing him to remain in power until 2036
Subject of an International Criminal Court arrest warrant (2023) for alleged deportation of Ukrainian children

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of Japan?
items.explanation:
Prime Minister of Japan (October 2025–present), Liberal Democratic Party
First woman to serve as Prime Minister of Japan
Also the first woman to lead the LDP as party president
Former Minister of Internal Affairs and Communications; known as a fiscal hawk
Elected by the National Diet on October 21, 2025, after winning the LDP leadership election
Suceeeded Shigeru Ishiba, who served briefly from October 2024
Considered a conservative nationalist; has visited the controversial Yasukuni Shrine
Defeated Shinjiro Koizumi (son of former PM Junichiro Koizumi) in the LDP leadership race

items.prompt: Who is the current President of South Korea?
items.explanation:
14th President of South Korea (June 2025–present), Democratic Party of Korea
Sworn in June 4, 2025, after winning the snap presidential election with 49.4% of the vote
Election triggered by the impeachment and removal of predecessor Yoon Suk-yeol
Yoon had declared martial law in December 2024—the Constitutional Court removed him in April 2025
Former Governor of Gyeonggi Province, South Korea's most populous province
Previously lost the 2022 presidential election to Yoon by 0.7%—the narrowest margin in Korean history
Pledged to reopen dialogue with North Korea and "heal wounds" after months of political turmoil

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of Australia?
items.explanation:
31st Prime Minister of Australia (2022–present), Australian Labor Party
Reelected in May 2025 with an expanded majority
Grew up in public housing in inner Sydney, raised by a single mother on a disability pension
First PM of Italian descent (father was Italian; Albanese visited Italy to meet him)
Served as Deputy PM and Infrastructure Minister under Kevin Rudd and Julia Gillard
Overseed the 2023 Voice to Parliament referendum (which failed) on Indigenous recognition
Known colloquially as "Albo"; first Labor PM to win reelection since Kevin Rudd in 2007

items.prompt: Who is the current President of South Africa?
items.explanation:
President of South Africa (2018–present), African National Congress (ANC)
Became president in 2018 after Jacob Zuma resigned amid corruption scandals
Won a second term after the 2024 elections, forming a Government of National Unity coalition
Key negotiator during the end of apartheid; led the ANC's negotiations with the de Klerk government
Former trade union leader and one of South Africa's wealthiest people through business investments
Survived the "Phala Phala" farmgate scandal (2022) involving cash found hidden on his farm
Protege of Nelson Mandela, who once considered him a potential presidential successor

items.prompt: Who is the current Prime Minister of Italy?
items.explanation:
Prime Minister of Italy (October 2022–present), Brothers of Italy (Fratelli d'Italia)
First woman to serve as Prime Minister of Italy
Leader of Brothers of Italy, a right-wing party with roots in Italy's post-fascist movement
Won the 2022 election in coalition with Matteo Salvini's League and Silvio Berlusconi's Forza Italia
At 45, was the youngest Italian PM at the time of her appointment
Has positioned herself as a pragmatic EU partner despite her party's euroskeptic origins
Berlusconi's death in 2023 made her the dominant figure in Italy's right-wing coalition
Maintained strong support for Ukraine, breaking with some European far-right peers
